fungal_infection.name: Fungal Infection
causes: Use of antibiotics disrupting natural flora
precautions: Avoid walking barefoot in public areas
basic_treatment: Keep affected area clean and dry
fungal_infection.when_to_see_doctor: If rash spreads rapidly, doesn't respond to OTC treatment after 2 weeks, or is accompanied by fever.

allergy.name: Allergy
precautions: Keep windows closed during high pollen seasons
allergy.when_to_see_doctor: If you experience difficulty breathing, throat swelling, or symptoms seriously impair daily life.

gerd.name: GERD (Acid Reflux)
precautions: Avoid lying down within 3 hours of eating
Elevate head of bed by 6-8 inches
gerd.when_to_see_doctor: If symptoms occur more than twice per week, medication isn't helping, or you have difficulty swallowing.

diabetes.name: Diabetes
causes: Type 1: Autoimmune destruction of insulin-producing cells
Type 2: Insulin resistance due to obesity, inactivity, genetics
basic_treatment: Type 2: Metformin, lifestyle changes, insulin if needed
Monitor and control blood pressure and cholesterol
diabetes.when_to_see_doctor: Immediately if blood sugar is very high (>300 mg/dL), you have severe symptoms, or experience diabetic ketoacidosis signs (vomiting, fruity breath, confusion).

hypertension.name: Hypertension (High Blood Pressure)
precautions: Manage stress through meditation or yoga
basic_treatment: ACE inhibitors, Beta-blockers, diuretics, calcium channel blockers (as prescribed)
hypertension.when_to_see_doctor: If BP consistently reads above 140/90 mmHg, or you have symptoms like severe headache, vision changes, or chest pain.

migraine.name: Migraine
migraine.when_to_see_doctor: If migraines are frequent (3+ per month), last more than 72 hours, or accompanied by high fever, stiff neck, or sudden severe headache.

malaria.name: Malaria
causes: Plasmodium parasite transmitted by Anopheles mosquito bites
precautions: Take prophylactic antimalarials when traveling to endemic areas
malaria.when_to_see_doctor: Immediately if you suspect malaria — it is a medical emergency. Seek care within 24 hours of symptom onset.

dengue.name: Dengue Fever
causes: Dengue virus transmitted by Aedes aegypti mosquito
precautions: Eliminate mosquito breeding sites (standing water in pots, tires)
basic_treatment: No specific antiviral treatment — supportive care
dengue.when_to_see_doctor: Urgently if you develop warning signs: abdominal pain, persistent vomiting, bleeding, or rapid breathing.

typhoid.name: Typhoid Fever
causes: Salmonella typhi bacteria via contaminated food or water
precautions: Drink only treated or bottled water
Wash hands before eating and after using toilet
Get typhoid vaccine before travel to high-risk areas
typhoid.when_to_see_doctor: Immediately if you suspect typhoid — requires antibiotic treatment to prevent serious complications.

common_cold.name: Common Cold
causes: Spread via respiratory droplets or contaminated surfaces
precautions: Avoid close contact with infected individuals
Don't touch face with unwashed hands
Boost immunity with vitamin C and zinc
common_cold.when_to_see_doctor: If symptoms worsen after 7 days, you have high fever (>39°C), severe throat pain, or difficulty breathing.

pneumonia.name: Pneumonia
pneumonia.when_to_see_doctor: Immediately if you have difficulty breathing, chest pain, high fever, or bluish lips — pneumonia can be life-threatening.

tuberculosis.name: Tuberculosis (TB)
causes: Spread through airborne droplets from active TB patients
precautions: Ensure good ventilation in living spaces
Complete full course of TB treatment
Isolate active TB patients until non-infectious
basic_treatment: 6-month course of antibiotics (isoniazid, rifampicin, ethambutol, pyrazinamide)
Never skip doses (risk of drug resistance)
tuberculosis.when_to_see_doctor: As soon as TB is suspected — it requires prompt diagnosis and a full supervised treatment course.

arthritis.name: Arthritis
causes: Rheumatoid arthritis: Autoimmune attack on joints
Osteoarthritis: Wear and tear of cartilage
precautions: Maintain healthy weight to reduce joint stress
arthritis.when_to_see_doctor: If joint pain is severe, you have sudden swelling, or symptoms significantly limit daily activity.

heart_attack.name: Heart Attack
causes: Blockage of coronary artery by blood clot
Risk factors: hypertension, diabetes, high cholesterol, smoking, obesity
symptoms: Pain radiating to arm, jaw, or back
precautions: Control blood pressure, cholesterol, and diabetes
basic_treatment: Aspirin if not allergic (while waiting for help)
heart_attack.when_to_see_doctor: This is a MEDICAL EMERGENCY. Call emergency services immediately at first signs — do not drive yourself.

hepatitis_a.name: Hepatitis A
causes: HAV virus via contaminated food or water
precautions: Wash hands thoroughly after toilet use and before food preparation
Avoid raw shellfish from contaminated water
basic_treatment: No specific antiviral treatment — supportive care
hepatitis_a.when_to_see_doctor: If you suspect hepatitis A — for testing, monitoring, and to protect others. Seek immediate care if you develop severe jaundice or confusion.

urinary_tract_infection.name: Urinary Tract Infection (UTI)
precautions: Avoid harsh soaps near genital area
Don't hold urine for long periods
basic_treatment: OTC pain relief (phenazopyridine) for symptom relief
Cranberry juice may help prevent recurrence
urinary_tract_infection.when_to_see_doctor: Immediately if you have high fever, back/flank pain, or shaking chills — may indicate kidney infection.

acne.name: Acne
precautions: Wash face twice daily with gentle cleanser
basic_treatment: Isotretinoin for severe cystic acne (prescription only)
acne.when_to_see_doctor: If OTC treatments haven't worked after 3 months, or if acne is severe, cystic, or causing significant distress.

varicose_veins.name: Varicose Veins
symptoms: Dark purple or blue veins visible under skin
varicose_veins.when_to_see_doctor: If you develop ulcers near ankles, severe swelling, or significant pain affecting daily life.
